Sremove_elements.solution:
Lemma Proper_eq_eq:
  forall f, Proper (E.eq ==> @eq bool) f.
Proof.
  unfold Proper, respectful.
  intros. unfold E.eq in H. subst. reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma Sremove_elements: forall (i: E.t) (s: S.t),
  S.In i s ->
     S.elements (S.remove i s) =
         List.filter (fun x => if E.eq_dec x i then false else true) (S.elements s).
Proof.
  intros.
  apply eqlistA_Eeq_eq.
  apply SortE_equivlistE_eqlistE.
  - apply S.elements_3.
  - apply filter_sortE. apply S.elements_3.
  - intro j.
    rewrite filter_InA; [| apply Proper_eq_eq].
    destruct (E.eq_dec j i).
    + split; intro.
      * destruct H0 as [H0 H1]. discriminate.
      * unfold E.eq in e. subst.
        exfalso. apply S.remove_1 with i s; auto.
        apply S.elements_2. assumption.
    + split; intro.
      * destruct H0 as [H0 _].
        apply S.elements_1. apply S.remove_3 with i.
        apply S.elements_2. assumption.
      * split.
        -- apply S.elements_1. apply S.remove_2; auto.
           apply S.elements_2. assumption.
        -- reflexivity.
Qed.
Sremove_elements.explanation: Uses SortE_equivlistE_eqlistE to reduce to showing equivalent element membership. Case split on whether j = i.

InA_map_fst_key.solution:
Proof.
  induction l; split; intros.
  - inv H.
  - destruct H as [e He]. inv He.
  - inv H.
    + destruct a. simpl in H1. exists a0. left. split; auto.
    + destruct (IHl) as [IH1 IH2]. destruct (IH1 H1) as [e He].
      exists e. right. assumption.
  - destruct H as [e He]. inv He.
    + destruct H0. simpl in *. left. assumption.
    + right. apply IHl. exists e. assumption.
Qed.
InA_map_fst_key.explanation: By induction on l with case analysis on InA. Maps between pair membership and first-element membership.

Sorted_lt_key.solution:
Proof.
  induction al; split; intros.
  - simpl. constructor.
  - constructor.
  - destruct a. simpl. inv H.
    constructor.
    + apply IHal. assumption.
    + clear IHal H2. induction al.
      * constructor.
      * inv H3. destruct a. simpl in *.
        constructor. assumption.
  - destruct a. simpl in *. inv H.
    constructor.
    + apply IHal. assumption.
    + clear IHal H2. induction al.
      * constructor.
      * inv H3. destruct a. simpl in *.
        constructor. assumption.
Qed.
Sorted_lt_key.explanation: By induction on al. Relates Sorted on pairs (via lt_key on first component) to Sorted on the map of first elements.

cardinal_map.solution:
Proof.
  intros.
  repeat rewrite M.cardinal_1.
  assert (Hlen: length (M.elements (M.map f g)) = length (M.elements g)).
  {
    assert (Hsorted1: Sorted (@M.lt_key B) (M.elements (M.map f g)))
      by apply M.elements_3.
    assert (Hsorted2: Sorted (@M.lt_key A) (M.elements g))
      by apply M.elements_3.
    apply Sorted_lt_key in Hsorted1.
    apply Sorted_lt_key in Hsorted2.
    assert (Hequiv: forall k, InA E.eq k (map fst (M.elements (M.map f g)))
                          <-> InA E.eq k (map fst (M.elements g))).
    {
      intro k. rewrite 2 InA_map_fst_key.
      split; intros [e He].
      - apply M.elements_2 in He.
        apply WF.map_mapsto_iff in He.
        destruct He as [a [Heq Hmap]].
        exists a. apply M.elements_1. assumption.
      - apply M.elements_2 in He.
        exists (f e). apply M.elements_1.
        apply WF.map_mapsto_iff.
        exists e. split; [reflexivity | assumption].
    }
    assert (HeqL: eqlistA E.eq
      (map fst (M.elements (M.map f g)))
      (map fst (M.elements g))).
    { apply SortE_equivlistE_eqlistE; auto. }
    apply eqlistA_length in HeqL.
    rewrite 2 length_map in HeqL.
    assumption.
  }
  assumption.
Qed.
cardinal_map.explanation: Show both element lists have the same length by proving their key lists are eqlistA via SortE_equivlistE_eqlistE. The equivalence of keys follows from WF.map_mapsto_iff.

Sremove_cardinal_less.solution:
Proof.
  intros.
  repeat rewrite S.cardinal_1.
  generalize (Sremove_elements _ _ H); intro.
  rewrite H0; clear H0.
  induction (S.elements s).
  - apply S.elements_1 in H. inv H.
  - simpl. destruct (E.eq_dec a i).
    + simpl. lia.
    + simpl. assert (S.In i (S.remove a (S.add a (S.of_list l)))).
      admit.
      Restart.
  intros. repeat rewrite S.cardinal_1.
  rewrite (Sremove_elements _ _ H).
  assert (InA E.eq i (S.elements s)) by (apply S.elements_1; auto).
  clear H.
  induction (S.elements s).
  - inv H0.
  - simpl. destruct (E.eq_dec a i).
    + simpl. lia.
    + inv H0. unfold E.eq in n. contradiction.
      simpl. specialize (IHl H1). lia.
Qed.
Sremove_cardinal_less.explanation: Rewrite with Sremove_elements, then show filtering removes exactly one element, decreasing the length.

Mremove_elements.solution:
Proof.
  intros.
  apply specialize_SortA_equivlistA_eqlistA.
  - apply M.elements_3.
  - apply filter_sort with (@M.eq_key_elt A).
    + apply M.eqke_equiv.
    + apply M.ltk_strorder.
    + apply Proper_eq_key_elt.
    + apply M.elements_3.
  - intro x.
    rewrite filter_InA; [| apply Proper_eq_key_elt].
    destruct (E.eq_dec (fst x) i).
    + split; intro.
      * destruct H0 as [H0 H1]. discriminate.
      * exfalso. destruct x. simpl in e.
        apply M.remove_1 with (x:=i) in e.
        -- contradiction.
        -- exists a. apply M.elements_2. assumption.
    + split; intro.
      * split.
        -- apply M.elements_1. apply M.remove_3 with i.
           apply M.elements_2. assumption.
        -- reflexivity.
      * destruct H0 as [H0 _].
        apply M.elements_1. apply M.remove_2; auto.
        apply M.elements_2. assumption.
Qed.
Mremove_elements.explanation: Same strategy as Sremove_elements but for maps. Uses specialize_SortA_equivlistA_eqlistA.

Mremove_cardinal_less.solution:
Proof.
  intros.
  repeat rewrite M.cardinal_1.
  pose proof (Mremove_elements A i s H).
  assert (length (M.elements (M.remove i s)) =
          length (List.filter (fun x => if E.eq_dec (fst x) i then false else true) (M.elements s))).
  { apply eqlistA_length with (@M.eq_key_elt A). assumption. }
  rewrite H1.
  destruct H as [v Hv].
  assert (InA (@M.eq_key_elt A) (i, v) (M.elements s)).
  { apply M.elements_1. assumption. }
  clear H0 H1 Hv.
  induction (M.elements s).
  - inv H2.
  - simpl. destruct (E.eq_dec (fst a) i).
    + simpl. lia.
    + inv H2.
      * destruct H0. simpl in *. contradiction.
      * simpl. specialize (IHl H0). lia.
Qed.
Mremove_cardinal_less.explanation: Use Mremove_elements and eqlistA_length, then show filtering removes one element from the list.

fold_right_rev_left.solution:
Proof.
  intros A B f l. induction l; intros.
  - simpl. reflexivity.
  - simpl. rewrite IHl.
    rewrite fold_right_app. simpl. reflexivity.
Qed.
fold_right_rev_left.explanation: By induction on l, using fold_right_app to relate fold_left to fold_right via reversal.

Snot_in_empty.solution:
Proof.
  intros n H. apply S.empty_1 in H. assumption.
Qed.
Snot_in_empty.explanation: Direct application of S.empty_1 which states nothing is in the empty set.

Sin_domain.solution:
Proof.
  intros A n g. unfold Mdomain.
  rewrite WP.fold_rec_bis.
  - split; intro.
    + apply Snot_in_empty in H. contradiction.
    + destruct H as [v Hv]. apply M.empty_1 in Hv. contradiction.
  - intros. rewrite <- H. assumption.
  - intros k v s' m' Hnotin Hmap [IH1 IH2].
    split; intro.
    + apply S.add_1 with (x := n) in H.
      Restart.
  intros A n g. unfold Mdomain.
  apply WP.fold_rec_bis.
  - intros m m' a Heq. split; intros.
    + rewrite <- Heq. assumption.
    + rewrite Heq. assumption.
  - split; intro.
    + exfalso. apply (Snot_in_empty n). assumption.
    + destruct H as [v Hv]. apply M.empty_1 in Hv. destruct Hv.
  - intros k e a m' Hnotin Hmap IH. split; intro.
    + destruct (E.eq_dec n k).
      * unfold E.eq in e0. subst. exists e. apply M.add_1. reflexivity.
      * apply S.add_3 in H; auto.
        apply IH in H. destruct H as [v Hv].
        exists v. apply M.add_2; auto.
    + destruct H as [v Hv].
      destruct (E.eq_dec n k).
      * unfold E.eq in e0. subst. apply S.add_1. reflexivity.
      * apply S.add_2.
        apply IH.
        exists v. apply M.add_3 with k e; auto.
        exact Hv.
Qed.
Sin_domain.explanation: Uses WP.fold_rec_bis to reason about M.fold. Three cases: the equivalence case, the base (empty) case, and the step case with S.add.

subset_nodes_sub.solution:
Proof.
  intros P g. unfold subset_nodes, nodes.
  apply WP.fold_rec_bis.
  - intros. rewrite <- H. assumption.
  - intros n. apply Snot_in_empty in H. contradiction.
  - intros k e a m Hnotin Hmap IH n Hin.
    destruct (P k e).
    + apply S.add_3 in Hin.
      * apply Sin_domain. apply IH in Hin.
        apply Sin_domain in Hin.
        destruct Hin as [v Hv].
        exists v. apply M.add_2; auto.
        intro. unfold E.eq in H. subst. apply Hnotin. exists v. assumption.
      * intro Heq. unfold E.eq in Heq. subst.
        Restart.
  intros P g. unfold subset_nodes, nodes.
  apply WP.fold_rec_bis.
  - intros m m' a Heq H n Hin. apply Sin_domain.
    rewrite <- Heq. apply Sin_domain. apply H. assumption.
  - intros n Hn. exfalso. apply (Snot_in_empty n Hn).
  - intros k e a m Hnotin Hmap IH n Hin.
    destruct (P k e).
    + destruct (E.eq_dec n k).
      * unfold E.eq in e0. subst.
        apply Sin_domain. exists e. apply M.add_1. reflexivity.
      * apply S.add_3 in Hin; auto.
        apply IH in Hin. apply Sin_domain in Hin.
        destruct Hin as [v Hv].
        apply Sin_domain. exists v. apply M.add_2; auto.
    + apply IH in Hin. apply Sin_domain in Hin.
      destruct Hin as [v Hv].
      apply Sin_domain. exists v. apply M.add_2; auto.
      intro. unfold E.eq in H. subst. apply Hnotin. exists v. assumption.
Qed.
subset_nodes_sub.explanation: Uses WP.fold_rec_bis. Shows that subset_nodes only produces elements that are keys in the graph.

select_terminates.solution:
Proof.
  intros K g n Hchoose.
  unfold remove_node.
  rewrite cardinal_map.
  apply Mremove_cardinal_less.
  assert (S.In n (subset_nodes (low_deg K) g)).
  { apply S.choose_1. assumption. }
  apply subset_nodes_sub in H.
  apply Sin_domain in H. assumption.
Qed.
select_terminates.explanation: Show n is in the graph domain via subset_nodes_sub and Sin_domain, then use Mremove_cardinal_less and cardinal_map.

adj_ext.solution:
Proof.
  intros g i j Hij.
  unfold E.eq in Hij. subst.
  apply S.eq_refl.
Qed.
adj_ext.explanation: Since E.eq is just equality on positives, subst makes i and j the same, and S.eq_refl finishes.

in_colors_of_1.solution:
Proof.
  intros i s f c Hi Hfind.
  unfold colors_of.
  apply S.fold_rec_bis.
  - intros. rewrite <- H. auto.
  - exfalso. apply (Snot_in_empty i Hi).
  - intros x a s' Hnotin Hmap IH.
    destruct (E.eq_dec i x).
    + unfold E.eq in e. subst.
      rewrite Hfind. apply S.add_1. reflexivity.
    + destruct (M.find x f).
      * apply S.add_2. apply IH.
        apply S.add_3 in Hi; auto.
      * apply IH. apply S.add_3 in Hi; auto.
      Restart.
  intros. unfold colors_of.
  revert H H0. revert i c.
  apply S.fold_rec_bis.
  - intros s0 s' a Heq IH i c Hi Hfind.
    apply IH; auto. rewrite Heq. assumption.
  - intros i c Hi. exfalso. apply (Snot_in_empty i Hi).
  - intros x a s' Hnotin Hmap IH i c Hi Hfind.
    destruct (E.eq_dec i x).
    + unfold E.eq in e. subst.
      rewrite Hfind. apply S.add_1. reflexivity.
    + assert (S.In i s') by (apply S.add_3 with x; auto).
      specialize (IH i c H Hfind).
      destruct (M.find x f).
      * apply S.add_2. assumption.
      * assumption.
Qed.
in_colors_of_1.explanation: Uses S.fold_rec_bis to reason about the fold in colors_of. When i=x, we find the color; otherwise use IH.

color_correct.solution:
Proof.
  intros palette g Hnoself Hundir.
  unfold color.
  set (K := S.cardinal palette).
  set (l := select K g).
  unfold coloring_ok.
  induction l as [| n l' IH].
  - simpl. intros i j Hadj. split.
    + intros ci Hfind. rewrite WF.empty_o in Hfind. discriminate.
    + intros ci cj Hfind. rewrite WF.empty_o in Hfind. discriminate.
  - simpl. unfold color1 at 1.
    set (f := fold_right (color1 palette g) (M.empty node) l').
    destruct (S.choose (S.diff palette (colors_of f (adj g n)))) eqn:Echoose.
    + intros i j Hadj. split.
      * intros ci Hfind.
        destruct (E.eq_dec i n).
        -- unfold E.eq in e0. subst.
           rewrite WF.add_eq_o in Hfind by reflexivity. inv Hfind.
           apply S.choose_1 in Echoose.
           apply S.diff_1 in Echoose. assumption.
        -- rewrite WF.add_neq_o in Hfind by auto.
           apply (IH i j Hadj). assumption.
      * intros ci cj Hfindi Hfindj.
        destruct (E.eq_dec i n); destruct (E.eq_dec j n).
        -- unfold E.eq in *. subst. exfalso. apply (Hnoself j). auto.
        -- unfold E.eq in e0. subst.
           rewrite WF.add_eq_o in Hfindi by reflexivity. inv Hfindi.
           rewrite WF.add_neq_o in Hfindj by auto.
           intro Heq. subst.
           apply S.choose_1 in Echoose.
           apply S.diff_2 in Echoose.
           apply Echoose.
           eapply in_colors_of_1.
           ++ exact Hadj.
           ++ exact Hfindj.
        -- unfold E.eq in e0. subst.
           rewrite WF.add_neq_o in Hfindi by auto.
           rewrite WF.add_eq_o in Hfindj by reflexivity. inv Hfindj.
           intro Heq. subst.
           apply S.choose_1 in Echoose.
           apply S.diff_2 in Echoose.
           apply Echoose.
           eapply in_colors_of_1.
           ++ apply Hundir. exact Hadj.
           ++ exact Hfindi.
        -- rewrite WF.add_neq_o in Hfindi by auto.
           rewrite WF.add_neq_o in Hfindj by auto.
           eapply IH; eauto.
    + apply IH.
Qed.
color_correct.explanation: By induction on the select list. Base case: empty coloring has no mappings, so all properties hold vacuously. Inductive case: unfold color1 and destruct S.choose. When a color e is chosen, it's in palette (from S.diff_1) and not in colors_of neighbors (from S.diff_2). The four sub-cases for (i=n, j=n) use these facts, Hundir (undirectedness), and the IH.
